description: Repository file inventory with classification: CANONICAL, ACTIVE, STALE, LEGACY, DEAD, UNKNOWN.

classification_rules.CANONICAL: Actively used by governance, replay, dashboards, attribution, or deploy (config.registry, dashboard.py, main.py, deploy_supervisor, EOD/cron).
classification_rules.ACTIVE: Used by scripts or tests (imported or invoked).
classification_rules.STALE: Not referenced in code in 90+ days or superseded by canonical path.
classification_rules.LEGACY: Old version of replaced system (e.g. shadow A/B, v1 composite).
classification_rules.DEAD: No references anywhere (never imported, never invoked).

stale_candidates: Reports under reports/ that are never read by dashboard or scripts (many one-off run artifacts).
archive/ (entire tree — legacy deployment and investigation scripts).
Root-level one-off scripts: RUN_*_NOW.py, FIX_*.py, COMPLETE_*.py, EXECUTE_*.py, many diagnose_*, investigate_*, fix_*.
legacy_candidates: shadow/ (if shadow A/B removed per MEMORY_BANK)
Old effectiveness_* dirs superseded by effectiveness_baseline_blame
unknown_review: moltbot/ (agents, memory_evolution — check if used by any workflow).
Root scripts that are invoked only manually (e.g. profitability_tracker.py, generate_daily_trading_report.py).
